start.title: Open project
ai.systemInstructions.description: System instructions are the basic rules and settings that define the model's behavior at a fundamental level. System instructions shape the model's «personality», its communication style, limitations, ethical principles, and overall approach to responses. For example, they can instruct the model to be helpful, truthful, witty, or to strictly adhere to certain safety rules. These instructions take priority over everything else.
ai.userInstructions.description: User instructions are specific requests, questions, or directives. They define exactly what the model should do in a given moment: answer a question, write text, analyze data, solve a problem, or complete a creative task. User instructions can be one-time or accumulate in the conversation context, but they always remain subordinate to the system instructions. It is the user instructions that allow directing the AI to solve the current task.
native.alert.title: Alert
native.confirm.title: Confirm
aiGenerationSettings.override: Specify custom AI generation settings for this node
fixProblemsNode.sourceNodeIdToFix.label: Source to fix
fixProblemsNode.sourceNodeIdToFix.descri: The source (one of the input nodes) where errors will be identified and corrected. You must select one source. Other inputs can be used for reference (e.g., style guide, writing goals, character reference, etc.).
fixProblemsNode.minSeverityToFix.label: Minimum error severity to fix
fixProblemsNode.minSeverityToFix.descrip: Since there can be multiple correction iterations, a stopping condition is needed. If none of the errors in the current set have a severity level equal to or higher than the specified one, the process will terminate.
fixProblemsNode.foundProblemsTemplate.la: Error list template name for instructions
fixProblemsNode.foundProblemsTemplate.de: Similar to how other node texts are inserted into instructions (using curly braces, e.g., {{Characters}}), the correction prompt requires a placeholder for the list of found errors. This field defines the template name. For example, if you enter 'Error List', you should use {{Error List}} in your instructions.
fixProblemsNode.maxIterations.label: Maximum iterations
fixProblemsNode.maxIterations.descriptio: The correction process will stop once the maximum number of iterations is reached, even if some important errors remain unfixed.
mergeNode.title: Merge Node Settings
planNode.title.label: Node Title
planNode.title.description: Short human-readable node title. Displayed in plan graph and also used as placeholder template name in linked nodes instructions ({{Title}}).
plan.overwrite_warning: Your manual changes will be overwritten. Regenerate?
plan.forEach.title: For‑Each Node
plan.forEach.description: Processes an array of texts in a loop. Contains internal input and output nodes.
plan.forEach.rawContentPlaceholder: Enter a JSON array of strings, e.g., ["text1", "text2"]
plan.forEach.rawContentDescription: The array that will be processed in the loop. Each element becomes an iteration.
